title: Quantum Dispersive Waves and Multimode Squeezing in Pure-Kerr Parametrically Driven Cavity Solitons
authors: Rafael Romero Mendez, Sashank Kaushik Sridhar, Samyak Gothi, Pradyoth Shandilya, Yichen Shen, Curtis Menyuk, Avik Dutt
abstract: Parametrically driven cavity solitons (PDCS), unlike single-pumped cavity solitons, are localized optical pulses arising from parametric processes. These cavity solitons, recently discovered in pure-Kerr media, offer great promise for nonlinear dynamics studies and metrology. Here, we present the first multimode quantum description of pure-Kerr PDCS. In the below threshold regime, we verify single- and two-mode squeezing, while above threshold we uncover novel "quantum" dispersive waves - the quantum analog of soliton Cherenkov radiation. Besides revealing these unexplored quantum properties, we show that PDCS generates up to 20 dB of squeezing, only limited by overcoupling and intrinsic losses for experimentally routine parameters. We therefore provide a pathway to observe strong multimode quantum noise reduction in these systems.
summary: This paper provides the first multimode quantum analysis of pure-Kerr parametrically driven cavity solitons. It demonstrates that these solitons can generate significant squeezing (up to 20 dB) and exhibit quantum-analogous dispersive waves. These findings offer a new way to achieve strong multimode quantum noise reduction for metrology and nonlinear dynamics.
main_problem: The study aims to provide the first multimode quantum description of pure-Kerr parametrically driven cavity solitons (PDCS) and explore their quantum noise properties.
main_result: The authors uncover single- and two-mode squeezing in the below-threshold regime and identify novel 'quantum' dispersive waves (the quantum analog of soliton Cherenkov radiation) above threshold, demonstrating up to 20 dB of squeezing.
method: The study employs a multimode quantum description of the nonlinear dynamics within the cavity.
model_or_system: The system consists of pure-Kerr parametrically driven cavity solitons (PDCS), which are localized optical pulses arising from parametric processes in a Kerr medium.
key_observables: Single-mode squeezing, two-mode squeezing, and quantum dispersive waves.
important_parameters: Below threshold and above threshold regimes; squeezing levels limited by overcoupling and intrinsic losses.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a pure-Kerr medium and considers losses primarily through overcoupling and intrinsic mechanisms.
paper_structure: The paper introduces PDCS, presents a multimode quantum description, analyzes squeezing in the below-threshold regime, investigates quantum dispersive waves above threshold, and discusses experimental feasibility regarding squeezing levels.
why_interesting: It is highly relevant for quantum optics and open quantum systems as it identifies a pathway to achieve high-level (20 dB) multimode squeezing and explores the quantum-classical correspondence in nonlinear wave phenomena.
